items.briefing:
Stolen passwords and compromised accounts have overtaken software vulnerabilities as the leading cause of ransomware attacks globally, according to new research. This means cybercriminals are increasingly breaking in by simply logging in with stolen credentials rather than exploiting technical flaws — making strong, unique passwords and multi-factor authentication more important than ever for all staff. Locally, both Microsoft and Cloudera are urging Australian organisations to strengthen their AI governance practices in line with the Australian Government's emerging AI framework.

**Practical tip:** Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on all your work accounts today — it's one of the most effective ways to stop unauthorised access even if your password is stolen.

featured.title: Cloudera urges Australian firms to tighten AI governance
featured.quote: Most Australian firms lack AI governance, creating urgent national security and compliance risks.
